setting.note: The Martian invasion mapped across real Surrey and London in strict order. The first cylinder lands in the sand-pits of Horsell Common at Woking; the tripods burn through Byfleet and are broken at the Thames by Shepperton and Weybridge; they push through Kew and Richmond into a London emptied by the exodus; and the last of them are found dead on Primrose Hill. Wells names the roads, stations, and church towers exactly. Horsell Common marks the landing with a tripod sculpture of its own.

rights.originalWork: Public domain. H. G. Wells, first published 1898; the author died in 1946.
rights.territoryNotes: Public domain in the United States (published 1898) and in the United Kingdom and the EU, where the author died more than 70 years ago (1946).
rights.attribution: H. G. Wells, The War of the Worlds (1898). Text from Project Gutenberg.

palette.note: The red weed the Martians carry from Mars, which swarms the Thames valley and turns the country crimson. Taken from the text and legible on paper at WCAG AA.

artilleryman.name: The artilleryman
artilleryman.colorSourceQuote: laid a brown hand upon my arm
